channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump invites Israel's Netanyahu to meet with him at the White House next week
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — President Donald Trump has invited Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House next week as the first foreign leader to visit in Trump's second term, Netanhayu and the White House said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How Donald Trump and Project 2025 previewed the federal grant freeze
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A White House order to freeze federal grants reflects a theory of presidential power that Donald Trump clearly endorsed during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The 'Mona Lisa' will get its own room under a major renovation of the Louvre
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron announced Tuesday that the “Mona Lisa” will get its own dedicated room inside the Louvre museum under a major renovation and expansion of the Paris landmark that will take up to a decade.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: Second California real estate agent charged with price gouging victims
item.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced that charges have been filed against another realtor for price gouging victims of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Firings, freezes and layoffs: A look at Trump's moves against federal employees and programs
item.description: CHAPIN, S.C. (AP) — Changeover among federal government agencies is normal when a new administration comes to Washington, with presidents regularly dismissing appointees selected by predecessors from opposing political parties.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Man stole car to smuggle migrants in trunk past Border Patrol checkpoint
item.description: A 19-year-old from Houston has been sentenced to three and a half years in federal prison for stealing a car that was used for human smuggling at the South Texas border, Acting U.S. Attorney Jennifer Lowery said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: (The Hill) -- One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Federal judge hears request to block an upcoming nitrogen gas execution in Alabama
item.description: A federal judge has heard competing testimony over a request to block the nation's fourth execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: Kim Chandler And Safiyah Riddle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The death toll from a New Year's Eve illegal fireworks explosion in Honolulu climbs to 5
item.description: A fifth person has died from injuries suffered during a massive explosion of illegal fireworks at a Honolulu home over New Year’s.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These are the most romantic restaurants in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tren de Aragua member wanted for Aurora, Colorado, apartment takeover busted in ICE raid in NYC
item.description: A Venezuelan migrant arrested Tuesday during a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement raid in New York City is a member of the Tren de Aragua gang, Fox News has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sean Duffy is confirmed by the Senate to lead the Transportation Department
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean Duffy was confirmed Tuesday as transportation secretary, giving him a key role in helping President Donald Trump cut regulations and fix the nation's infrastructure. The former Wisconsin congressman has promised safer Boeing planes, less regulation and help for U.S.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: "We are devastated by the accident involving a team member at Charlotte International Airport (CLT)," a spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, NewsNation

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Indiana man pardoned by Trump is fatally shot during a traffic stop
item.description: RENSSELAER, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man was fatally shot by a deputy during a traffic stop just days after he was pardoned by President Donald Trump for a misdemeanor related to the 2021 U.S. Capitol riot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shooting at Indiana grocery store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: When police “engaged” the shooting suspect outside the store, two officers were wounded and the suspect was fatally shot, according to the Elkhart Police Department.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No one injured after Border Patrol, cartel shootout in Texas
item.description: (NewsNation) — U.S. Border Patrol was involved in a shootout with cartel members in the Rio Grande Valley, the Texas Department of Public Safety says. The tense moments unfolded Monday in Fronton, Texas. At around 1:40 p.m, shots were fired from the Mexican side of the border at agents.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Border agents shot at in South Texas
item.description: Border Patrol agents were fired upon Monday in the remote Starr County town of Fronton, Texas, Border Report has been told.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Egg prices are soaring. Don't expect that to change anytime soon
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Bird flu is forcing farmers to slaughter millions of chickens a month, pushing U.S. egg prices to more than double their cost in the summer of 2023. And it appears there may be no relief in sight, given the surge in demand as Easter approaches.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, MARK VANCLEAVE and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Shooting at Indiana store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: ELKHART, Ind. (AP) — A shooting at a grocery store in northern Indiana on Monday left three people dead, including the suspected shooter, and two police officers wounded, officials said. Police received a 911 call at about 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. public health officials have been told to stop working with the World Health Organization, effective immediately. A U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Super Bowl footballs are ready to go within hours of the matchup being set
item.description: As soon as this year's Super Bowl matchup was set, workers at the Wilson Sporting Goods football factory jumped into action. The factory in the rural village of Ada, Ohio, makes the game balls used by every NFL team along with many of the nation’s top college programs and high schools.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Coke jumps on nostalgic trend with new Coca-Cola Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Trump's first executive actions on climate and environment
item.description: President Donald Trump's first week in office included a flurry of executive orders with implications for Earth's climate and environment.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, KRON4

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mahomes and the Chiefs will face Hurts, Barkley and the Eagles as they seek a Super Bowl three-peat
item.description: Patrick Mahomes, Travis Kelce and the Kansas City Chiefs are going for a three-peat in a Super Bowl rematch. This time, Jalen Hurts and the Philadelphia Eagles have Saquon Barkley. The Eagles and Chiefs will face off for the Lombardi Trophy for the second time in three years on Feb.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ryan Pressly waives no-trade clause to join the Chicago Cubs, AP source says
item.description: The Chicago Cubs are finalizing a trade for Ryan Pressly, adding the right-hander to the back end of their bullpen in their second major offseason deal with the Houston Astros.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Penske's pair of Porsches dominate as The Captain wins Rolex 24 at Daytona for 2nd consecutive year
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Roger Penske started his 59th season in motorsports the same way he closed last year — as a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pumps coal as answer to AI power needs but any boost could be short-lived
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s promise to go all in on fossil fuels includes praise for coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that's long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4
